events.description: President Joe Biden was inaugurated and established a federal goal of administering 100 million vaccine doses within his first 100 days in office.
The FDA granted Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for the Johnson & Johnson (Janssen) single-dose COVID-19 vaccine, expanding access options.
Public health data indicated a significant decline in vaccine hesitancy across most demographic groups, particularly among young adults and Black Americans.

stakeholders.name: Jared Polis
stakeholders.position: Advocated for widespread vaccination efforts and emphasized the importance of reaching rural and hesitant populations to achieve state health goals.

stakeholders.name: Jill Hunsaker Ryan
stakeholders.role: Executive Director, Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment
stakeholders.position: Publicly addressed the need to overcome vaccine hesitancy by focusing on community-based outreach and addressing specific safety concerns among skeptical residents.

outcome_description: Throughout 2021, vaccine hesitancy in the United States generally declined across most demographic groups, though it remained persistent in specific populations despite increased availability and public health outreach.
outcome_source: Kaiser Family Foundation (KFF) COVID-19 Vaccine Monitor

key_figures.republican_vaccination_intent: 56% had received or planned to receive the vaccine
